name: Speedy Cash
description_short: Speedy Cash Arlington, Texas — Speedy Cash in Memphis offers payday loans up to $425, lines of credit up to $4,000, and title loans up to $4,000, plus c...
description_long:
Speedy Cash is a national short-term lending chain with a retail location at 5126 Summer Avenue in Memphis, Tennessee. The store operates six days a week, closing only on Sundays, and offers both in-store and online application options. The brand markets itself as a neighborhood financial services provider for consumers who need quick access to cash outside of traditional banking channels.

The Memphis Summer Avenue location offers three loan products: Payday Loans up to $425 (repaid on the borrower's next pay date), Lines of Credit up to $4,000 (a revolving draw account repaid over time), and Title Loans up to $4,000 (secured by the borrower's vehicle after an in-store appraisal). Beyond lending, the store provides money orders, wire transfers, check cashing, and Green Dot Visa Debit Card services, making it a one-stop shop for consumers who lack or prefer not to use a bank account.

The in-store application model is a deliberate differentiator for customers who find online-only lenders confusing or impersonal. Staff walk applicants through loan options, documentation requirements, and repayment terms before signing. For title loans, the vehicle is appraised on-site during the visit. The store also posts its Tennessee-specific Rates & Terms page, which is a legal requirement and allows borrowers to review costs before committing.

Speedy Cash fills a genuine gap for cash-strapped consumers with few options, but it comes at a cost. Payday loans and title loans carry high APRs that are not disclosed on the store page itself — borrowers must navigate to the state Rates & Terms page to see them. Title loans put a vehicle at risk of repossession if the borrower defaults. The store's copy also contains a reference to 'Check Into Cash' — a competing brand — suggesting content inconsistencies that may confuse some visitors. Speedy Cash is best used as a short-term bridge, not a recurring borrowing strategy.
pros: Payday loans available up to $425 for small, fast cash needs
Line of Credit up to $4,000 provides flexible, revolving access to funds
Title loans up to $4,000 for borrowers who own a vehicle outright
Multiple financial services under one roof: check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, Green Dot Debit Card
In-store staff guide applicants through the full process and can answer questions before signing
Online application option available for borrowers who prefer not to visit in person
Extended hours Monday and Friday until 7pm for working borrowers
cons: Payday loan cap is low — only $425 — limiting usefulness for larger emergencies
APR and fee rates are not shown on the store page; borrowers must seek the state Rates & Terms link separately
Title loans require pledging a vehicle as collateral, creating repossession risk on default
Closed on Sundays, limiting access on weekends
Store page contains a 'Check Into Cash' brand reference, indicating sloppy content management that may erode trust
best_for: Employed borrowers needing a small cash advance before their next paycheck
Consumers who own a paid-off vehicle and need $1,000–$4,000 quickly
Unbanked or underbanked individuals needing check cashing or money services alongside a loan
Borrowers who prefer face-to-face guidance over online-only application processes
diagnosis: Speedy Cash is best for Memphis-area consumers who need same-day cash in amounts under $4,000 and either lack bank access or prefer in-person service. The main caveat is cost: payday and title loan APRs are typically triple-digit, and title loans carry repossession risk — this should be a last-resort tool, not a routine one.
services: In-store loan application with staff assistance
Vehicle appraisal for title loan collateral
